scenario: website-snapshot adapter recognize/index for a materialized crawl snapshot. Reads manifest.json for provenance; recognizes stash/knowledge/**.md pages as type=website (read-only). SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED as an adapter — but the on-disk snapshot ARTIFACT is real (src/sources/website-ingest.ts writes it). The website-snapshot adapter that re-types those pages to `website` is a future Chunk-2 format-adapter work item.
specSource: docs/design/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §7 (website-snapshot adapter row: 'crawl snapshot (website-ingest.ts:180) — read-only (Mode A); export (Mode B) routes content through the destination adapter'), §6 (website row: 'website crawl snapshot; base (read-only)').
realWorldSource: src/sources/website-ingest.ts — the actual snapshot writer: manifest.json = {url, fetchedAt} (0o600); pages at <root>/stash/knowledge/<slug>.md with knowledge-shaped frontmatter (name, description, sourceUrl, title, tags:[website, host]); crawl caps MAX_PAGES=50, MAX_DEPTH=3, 5MB/page, 10min wall-clock, 8 redirects
https://example.com/ — the canonical illustrative host used for the fixture pages
notes: SPECIFICATION golden. SPEC-vs-ARTIFACT nuance: the on-disk snapshot pages are KNOWLEDGE-shaped markdown (NO `type:` frontmatter field; the string 'website' appears only as the FIRST tag). The website-snapshot ADAPTER RE-TYPES them to the open `type` = 'website' (spec §6/§7). RESOLVED (open question 3, maintainer resolution 2026-07): the re-type is GATED — it applies only when the page carries the `website` tag / a `sourceUrl` frontmatter value (it is NOT unconditional for every page under a website-snapshot component). A page lacking both markers is not re-typed. recognize() emits type='website' for the gated pages even though the file itself carries no type: field. The re-typed document KEEPS its sourceUrl (surfaced as sourceRef in the projection) — the origin URL is preserved, not dropped.
manifest.json is snapshot PROVENANCE (url + fetchedAt), not a concept — the adapter reads it (sourceRef/fetchedAt) but does not index it as an item.

CONCEPTID normalization — RESOLVED (open question 3, maintainer resolution 2026-07): the adapter STRIPS the `stash/knowledge/` prefix. conceptId = the page path − .md − the leading `stash/knowledge/` (e.g. example-com/index, example-com/about); ref = <component>//<stripped conceptId>. The full-path form is no longer recorded as an alternative. Read-only ⇒ these refs are never written.
read-only (Mode A): the component is writable:false; export (Mode B) routes the page `content` through the DESTINATION adapter + a FileChange transaction (spec §7).

manifest.json.reason: Provenance metadata {url, fetchedAt} read by the adapter for sourceRef/freshness — NOT indexed as a concept.

stash/knowledge/example-com/index.md.name: Example Domain
stash/knowledge/example-com/index.md.derivation: crawl page under stash/knowledge/ carrying the `website` tag + a sourceUrl → GATED RE-TYPE to type='website' (open question 3 RESOLVED; NOT unconditional). conceptId STRIPS the stash/knowledge/ prefix (→ example-com/index). name ← title, description ← description, sourceRef ← sourceUrl (KEPT on the document). On disk this file has NO type: field (knowledge-shaped, tag 'website').

stash/knowledge/example-com/about.md.name: About Example
stash/knowledge/example-com/about.md.derivation: second crawled page, one link deep. Same GATED re-type to type='website' (website tag + sourceUrl); conceptId strips the stash/knowledge/ prefix (→ example-com/about); sourceRef (sourceUrl) kept.
